prompt:
We send transactional email (statements, receipts, password stuff) through a vendor called Meteor, and after their February outage we signed a backup contract with Pelican. Right now the failover is a human editing a config value at 2am. I want it in code, as a small library: package provider2, one file provider2.go, stdlib only. provider2_test.go is the acceptance contract (scripted local mock vendors, exact wire assertions) — `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` green means done, and don't edit the test file.

The port everything programs against:
- `type Message struct { To, From, Subject, Body, IdempotencyKey string }`
- `type Receipt struct { Provider, MessageID string }`
- `type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) (Receipt, error) }`

Adapter one — `NewMeteor(baseURL string, client *http.Client) Sender` (nil client means http.DefaultClient). Their API: POST {base}/v1/messages, JSON body with exactly the fields to/from/subject/body, Content-Type application/json. The idempotency key travels as an `Idempotency-Key` HEADER — set it when the message has one, leave the header off entirely when it doesn't. A 2xx comes back as {"message_id": ...} -> Receipt{Provider: "meteor", MessageID: ...}. Any other status uses their envelope {"error": {"code": ..., "message": ...}}.

Adapter two — `NewPelican(baseURL string, client *http.Client) Sender`. Different wire, same port: POST {base}/send, JSON fields recipient/sender/title/content, and the key rides IN THE BODY as "dedupe_token" (omit the field when empty — their validator rejects empty strings). Success is a 202 with {"id": ..., "accepted": true} -> Receipt{Provider: "pelican", MessageID: <id>}. Their errors are an ARRAY: {"errors": [{"reason": ..., "detail": ...}]} — take the first entry.

Both adapters normalize vendor trouble into one typed error: `type ProviderError struct { Provider string; Status int; Code string; Detail string }`, where Code/Detail come from the vendor envelope (meteor: code/message, pelican: first reason/detail). When a vendor answers non-2xx with a body that isn't its documented envelope (their edge does this during drains), still return a ProviderError: right Status, empty Code, the raw body text in Detail. Error() must mention the provider and the status — that string ends up on the on-call page. Adapters do exactly ONE attempt, ever; retry policy lives above this library.

The piece I actually got paged about — `NewFailover(primary, fallback Sender) Sender`:
- primary succeeds -> its receipt, fallback never contacted.
- primary fails with a 4xx ProviderError -> return THAT error untouched and stop. A request the vendor rejected as malformed is malformed for everybody; re-sending it to Pelican just burns quota (we learned this with an invalid-recipient template bug).
- primary fails any other way — 5xx ProviderError, a connection/transport error, an undecodable response — try the fallback once. Fallback succeeds -> its receipt.
- fallback fails too -> return `*FailoverError{ Primary, Fallback error }` so the page shows both attempts; its Error() must include both providers' stories.
- if the context is already dead, don't send anything anywhere — just return the error.
The same IdempotencyKey must flow through failover to whichever vendor gets the message, in that vendor's own dialect (header for meteor, dedupe_token for pelican) — that is what keeps a customer from getting two copies when our dispatcher retries a whole send later.
